ogTitle: Yimbun vs Yorkeys Knob: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Yimbun and Yorkeys Knob.
heading: Yimbun vs Yorkeys Knob — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Yimbun, Queensland and Yorkeys Knob, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Yorkeys Knob has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Yimbun (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Yimbun or Yorkeys Knob safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Yimbun has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Yorkeys Knob scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Yimbun scores 63/100 for bushfire risk. Yorkeys Knob scores 26/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Yimbun or Yorkeys Knob?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Yorkeys Knob has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
